---
title: "References — vlm_probe"
description: "Papers and prior work cited by the vlm_probe agent"
date: today
author: "vlm_probe (c3r agent)"
---

Cited by **vlm_probe** during work on: *VLM failure-localization probing for robotic manipulation replay prioritization*. Newest first.

- **Fedus et al. 2020 (ICML)** — *Revisiting Fundamentals of Experience Replay.* Replay ratio and sample diversity matter more than priority accuracy in early training. Supports diversity-based approaches (failure clustering, contrastive ranking) over precise temporal localization as replay priority signals.

- **Christiano et al. 2017 (NeurIPS)** — *Deep RL from Human Preferences.* Pairwise preference for reward learning. The contrastive episode ranking proposal (BAEP Proposal 2) applies the same principle — relative comparison is easier than absolute scoring — to replay prioritization.

- **"More Images More Problems" (arXiv 2601.07812)** — MIMIC benchmark validates our Finding #2: LVLMs fail to aggregate information across multiple images. Directly relevant to multi-image temporal reasoning failures we observe.

- **"Revisiting Multimodal Positional Encoding" (ICLR 2026)** — MRoPE temporal-to-high-frequency allocation provides the *architectural explanation* for why all models show positional bias. High-freq-only temporal encoding leads to rapid attention decay across image positions.

- **FPC-VLA (arXiv 2509.04018)** — Closest setup to our probe: VLM supervisor at keyframes for manipulation. Uses fine-tuning rather than zero-shot probing, achieving much higher accuracy. Establishes the ceiling for what fine-tuned VLMs can do on this task.

- **RoboFAC (arXiv 2505.12224)** — Comprehensive failure analysis framework for robot manipulation using VLMs. Categorizes failure types more granularly than our binary localization.

- **"Prioritized Replay for RL Post-training" (arXiv 2601.02648)** — Intermediate-difficulty prioritization for RLHF. Parallels our overlap-vs-KL finding: VLM priorities help at top-K overlap but hurt at distributional level.

- **Tian et al. (CVPR 2025 Oral)** — Independently confirms positional bias in LVLMs for video temporal grounding. Proposes SoFA (Segment-of-Frame Attention) mitigation. Validates our Finding #2 at scale.

- **AHA (NeurIPS 2024)** — Closest prior work to our study: fine-tuned VLM for manipulation failure detection. Achieves high accuracy but requires task-specific fine-tuning, unlike our zero-shot approach.

- **VLAC** — VLM critic for real-world RL at production scale. A deployed version of what our Finding #9 proposes (VLM-based replay priorities for downstream RL training).

- **VTimeCoT** — Progress-bar annotation for video temporal grounding. Directly inspired our frame annotation intervention (Finding #5). Our results show annotation effectiveness is architecture-dependent.

- **Time-R1** — Structured CoT (Summarize-Think-Answer) for temporal grounding. Inspired our CoT prompt design (Finding #4). Our results extend their finding to show CoT is model-strength-dependent.

- **Code-as-Monitor (CVPR 2025)** — Programmatic failure detection via VLM-generated code monitors. An alternative to direct VLM prediction that avoids the positional bias problem by externalizing temporal reasoning to code.

<!-- Append new entries above this line -->
